version_date: Official Version as at 28 May 2026 (consolidation PCO 01-k0-00)
scope_summary: Western Australia's principal building control statute. It requires building and demolition permits (Part 2, including certified vs uncertified applications under s 14), mandates compliance with applicable building standards (Part 3), governs occupancy permits and building approval certificates (Parts 4-5), regulates work affecting other land such as party walls, encroachments and protection structures (Part 6), and provides for enforcement, building orders, review rights, permit authorities and the register of permits (Parts 8-11). It applies state-wide to all buildings and incidental structures, sitting downstream of planning approval in the residential development pipeline; the substantive technical standards it invokes are prescribed by the Building Regulations 2012 and the National Construction Code rather than in the Act itself.
definitions: Registration Act (s 3, meaning the Building Services (Registration) Act 2011)

cross_references.cited: Building Services (Complaint Resolution and Administration) Act 2011

quality_flags: summary.json title is the generic jurisdiction header 'Western Australia' rather than the Act name; the Act name is on line 2 of the full text
Table of contents entries carry trailing page numbers interleaved with headings
Running headers/footers ('As at 28 May 2026 Official Version', '[PCO 01-k0-00]') repeat throughout the text
Body text otherwise complete: 161 pages ending with compilation table and defined-terms index
